query: How frequently in 2026 has Russia launched overnight drone barrages against Ukraine exceeding 600 drones in a single night? List specific dates and drone counts for the largest barrages in May, June, and early July 2026 according to Ukrainian Air Force figures.
answer:
## Summary

I researched Russian overnight drone barrages against Ukraine in 2026, and the key finding **corrects a common misconception embedded in the question**:

**Russian single-night barrages exceeding 600 drones against Ukraine were rare in 2026 — essentially just one mid-May episode.** The widely reported 600+ drone figures from June 2026 (660 drones on June 18 and June 26) were **Ukrainian attacks *on* Russia**, not Russian attacks on Ukraine.

### By period (Ukrainian Air Force / Zelensky figures):

**May 2026 — the peak:**
- **May 13–14, 2026**: Russia's largest assault of the war — a ~30-hour, multi-stage campaign totaling **1,567 drones + 56 missiles** ("more than 1,560 drones," per Zelensky).
- The discrete **overnight wave of May 14** was **~675 Shahed-type UAVs** (part of ~731 drones-and-missiles that night) — the clearest single-night Russian barrage over 600 drones in 2026.

**June 2026 — none over 600:**
- Russia launched **5,749 Shahed UAVs across the whole month** (~191/night average, per the ISIS tracker). No single Russian night reached 600.
- Example: night of June 26 was only **189 drones + 7 missiles**.

**Early July 2026 — below threshold:**
- **July 1–2**: ~**500 drones + 70+ missiles** (a deadly Kyiv strike, but under 600 drones).
- July 9: ~94 drones; July 10–11: ~121 drones.

**Frequency conclusion:** Through mid-July 2026, effectively **one** episode (May 13–14) saw a Russian overnight wave reach/exceed ~600 drones. Also worth flagging: the famous **728-drone record was July 9, 2025 — not 2026.**

Full structured findings (key facts, sources, and quantitative data points) have been submitted.

sources.title: Russia hammers Ukraine in biggest prolonged drone attack since war began

sources.title: 14 May 2026 Russian strikes on Ukraine
sources.snippet: During the night of 13-14 May 2026, Russia conducted its 'largest aerial attack' since the invasion: 1,567 drones and 56 missiles; 27 civilians dead.

sources.title: Russia Launches Record 1,600-Drone and Missile Attack on Ukraine
sources.snippet: May 13 day: 753 drones; night May 13: 138; night May 14: 731 drones and missiles incl. 675 Shahed-type UAVs + 56 missiles; 30-hour total 1,600+.

sources.title: Russia Launches Drone and Missile Attack on Kyiv in Deadly 'Night of Horror'
sources.snippet: Zelensky: Russia launched more than 70 missiles, nearly half ballistic, along with almost 500 attack drones overnight July 1-2, 2026.

sources.title: Ukraine unleashes one of its heaviest drone bombardments, as Russia strikes Ukraine
sources.snippet: Ukraine's defenses overnight June 26 stopped 174 of 189 Russian drones; four of seven Iskander-M missiles got through.

sources.title: Monthly Analysis of Russian Shahed 136 Deployment Against Ukraine - June 2026
sources.snippet: June 2026: Russia launched 5,749 Shahed-type UAVs including decoys; a clear break from Jan-May rapid growth pattern.

sources.title: Russia reports downing 660 Ukrainian drones
sources.snippet: 660-drone attack was one of Kyiv's biggest of the war — a Ukrainian attack on Russia, not a Russian attack on Ukraine.

sources.title: Russian missile and drone barrage kills eight and wounds dozens in Ukraine
sources.snippet: Russia hit Kyiv with ballistic missiles and 121 drones on July 10-11, 2026; at least eight killed.

sources.title: On the night of July 9, Russia attacked Ukraine with missiles and nearly a hundred drones
sources.snippet: Night of July 9, 2026: two Iskander-M ballistic missiles and 94 drones; 72 targets neutralized.

sources.title: Russia batters Ukraine with more than 700 drones, the largest barrage of the war
sources.snippet: 728 drones and 13 missiles record was July 9, 2025 (not 2026).

sources.title: Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 2, 2026
sources.snippet: Russian forces conducted a large drone and missile strike series against Ukraine on the night of July 1-2, primarily targeting Kyiv.

sources.title: Russian Army Downs Nearly 700 Ukrainian Drones Overnight
sources.snippet: Russia's MoD said it downed 660 Ukrainian drones on June 26 — a Ukrainian strike on Russia.

key_facts: Russia's largest aerial assault of the war was the May 13-14, 2026 campaign: ~1,567 drones and 56 missiles over ~30 hours (Zelensky: 'more than 1,560 drones'), the largest since the invasion.
The discrete overnight wave of May 14, 2026 comprised ~675 Shahed-type UAVs (part of ~731 drones-and-missiles that night) — the clearest single-night Russian barrage exceeding 600 drones in 2026.
In all of June 2026, Russia launched 5,749 Shahed-type UAVs total (~191/night average per ISIS tracker) — no single Russian night exceeded 600 drones.
The 660-drone barrages of June 18 and June 26, 2026 were UKRAINIAN attacks ON Russia, not Russian attacks on Ukraine — a common point of confusion.
The night of July 1-2, 2026 involved ~500 attack drones + 70+ missiles against Ukraine (Zelensky) — below the 600-drone threshold; killed 25-31 in Kyiv.
The night of June 26, 2026 Russian attack on Ukraine was only 189 drones + 7 Iskander-M missiles per Ukrainian Air Force.
The famous 728-drone single-night record was July 9, 2025 — NOT 2026.
July 10-11, 2026 Russian attack was ~121 drones plus ballistic missiles; killed at least eight.
Through mid-July 2026, effectively only one episode (May 13-14) saw a Russian overnight wave reach/exceed ~600 drones against Ukraine.
The May 14, 2026 overnight wave included 35 Kh-101 cruise missiles, 18 Iskander-M ballistic missiles, and 3 Kinzhal aeroballistic missiles alongside the drones.
